learnings.text: File locking with fcntl provides simple, reliable atomicity for concurrent task claiming
Injecting instructions into briefs is more flexible than requiring template files
Pool exhaustion handling is critical for graceful worker termination
Forward Broadcast enables async knowledge sharing between Drops without coordination overhead
Hypothesis Racing provides clean competition mechanism for parallel theory testing
Delegate-Only Mode enforces clear provenance and prevents orchestrator interference
Task Pool enables dynamic work distribution for uniform tasks without pre-planning
Integration tests require careful isolation using temporary directories and module patching
Testing parallel features like task pool claiming can be simulated without actual concurrency
Flexible test configuration keys improve backward compatibility
First-wins pattern is useful for debugging scenarios where multiple parallel theories need testing
Superseded Drops should be clearly distinguished from failed/dead Drops since they were actively canceled
The verdict field provides a clean way for Drops to signal hypothesis confirmation vs rejection
